messages.content: in c# separate a string by ";" into an array of strings

To separate a string by ";" into an array of strings in C#, you can use the `Split()` method from the `String` class. Here is an example:

```csharp
string input = "example1;example2;example3;example4";
string[] result = input.Split(';');

foreach(string s in result)
{
    Console.WriteLine(s);
}
```

In this example, the `Split(';')` method is used to split the `input` string into an array of strings, using ";" as the delimiter. Each substring is stored in the `result` array.

Then, you can iterate over the `result` array using a foreach loop to print each separated string.

The output will be:
```
example1
example2
example3
example4
```

Note: You need to include the `System` namespace at the start of your code file to use the `String` class.
I have a Calendar control in a c# project and I want to mark some days in the calendar, because those days are important, how can I select those days marking them?
Calendar doesn't have a method called AddBoldedDate nor UpdateBoldedDates

how to prevent clicks on the calendar? I have selected dates I want to keep selected so I don't want the user to click on the dates. i tried enabled but it grayout everything, and it doesn't have a readonly property
I want to make any date to not be clickable

If you want to make all dates in the Calendar control not clickable, you can simply set the `Enabled` property of the control to false. Here's an example:

```csharp
calendarControl.Enabled = false;
```

By setting `Enabled` to false, all dates in the Calendar control will be grayed out and the user won't be able to select or click on any date.

However, please note that by doing this, you won't be able to programmatically select or interact with the Calendar control either. If you need to programmatically select dates while preventing user interaction, you can use a different approach like overlaying a transparent panel or disabling only specific dates as shown in the previous examples.
without using enabled
it says it can't find the property DayRender in Calendar control
calendar wpf control doesn't have any of those methods
ir works perfect, but I do want the user to change the month, how can I prevent click on the dates while allowing the user to change the month
it works perfect, can you tell me how to obtain the current displayed year and month in this calendar?

To obtain the current displayed year and month in the `Calendar` control, you can access the `DisplayDate` property of the control. Here's an example:

```csharp
int currentYear = calendarControl.DisplayDate.Year;
int currentMonth = calendarControl.DisplayDate.Month;
```

In this example, the `DisplayDate` property of the `Calendar` control is accessed to get the currently displayed date. The `Year` and `Month` properties of the `DateTime` object are then used to obtain the current year and month, respectively.

You can use these variables (`currentYear` and `currentMonth`) to perform any necessary operations or display the current year and month as needed in your application.
